question_text: Which statement best describes the relationship between the angle of the Sun in the sky and the length of daylight during the year?

options.label: A
options.text: When the Sun is at a higher angle in the sky, daylight is shorter because the Sun travels faster

options.label: B
options.text: When the Sun is at a higher angle in the sky, daylight is longer because the hemisphere is tilted more directly towards the Sun, and this same tilt extends daylight hours

options.label: C
options.text: The angle of the Sun and day length are unrelated; they change for different reasons

options.label: D
options.text: When the Sun is at a lower angle, daylight is longer because the light has to travel further through the atmosphere

explanation: Both the angle of the Sun and day length increase together due to the same cause: the tilt of Earth's axis. When a hemisphere tilts towards the Sun, the Sun reaches a higher angle at noon AND daylight hours are longer. Both are consequences of the same axial tilt.
